questions.questionText: The longest and heaviest bone in the body is the:
questions.explanation: The femur is the longest and strongest bone.

questions.questionText: Which of the following is a sesamoid bone?
questions.explanation: Patella (largest) and pisiform are sesamoid bones developing within tendons.

questions.questionText: The primary ossification center for long bones typically appears in the:
questions.explanation: Primary ossification occurs in the diaphysis during fetal life.

questions.questionText: The lateral malleolus is a feature of which bone?
questions.explanation: The distal end of the fibula forms the lateral malleolus.

questions.questionText: The odontoid process (dens) is a distinguishing feature of the:
questions.explanation: The dens projects upward from the body of the axis.

questions.questionText: Which bones form the hard palate?
questions.explanation: Palatine process of maxilla and horizontal plate of palatine bone.

questions.questionText: The acetabulum is formed by the union of:
questions.explanation: All three innominate bones contribute to the acetabulum.

questions.questionText: The coracoid process is a feature of the:
questions.explanation: The coracoid process projects anteriorly from the scapula.

questions.questionText: Fracture of the surgical neck of the humerus endangers the:
questions.explanation: Axillary nerve and posterior circumflex humeral artery lie here.

questions.questionText: The pterion is an important landmark on the skull. It overlies the:
questions.optionB: Anterior branch of middle meningeal artery
questions.explanation: The pterion overlies the anterior division of the MMA.

questions.questionText: In Osteology, which statement is most accurate regarding the core definition and diagnostic concept?
questions.optionA: It reflects the most exam-relevant definition used in standard Anatomy texts
questions.optionB: It is an obsolete definition no longer used in clinical practice
questions.optionC: It is used only in pediatric population and never in adults
questions.optionD: It has no diagnostic significance in NEET-PG pattern questions
questions.explanation: This tests foundational definition-level knowledge from standard Anatomy references for Osteology. Subject: Anatomy. Chapter: Osteology.

questions.questionText: The most important etiology/risk factor to suspect first in a typical Osteology scenario is:
questions.optionA: The common high-yield risk factor repeatedly asked in entrance exams
questions.optionB: A rare cause seen only in isolated case reports
questions.optionD: An etiology excluded by routine first-line workup
questions.explanation: NEET-PG and INI-CET focus on common, high-yield causes before rare differentials. Subject: Anatomy. Chapter: Osteology.
